summary: Backend glossary API fully tested and PASSING (5/5 tests). Frontend: welcome screen + send works, but GlossaryText highlighting is BROKEN - assistant response text containing Bitcoin/Blockchain/DeFi/Wallet/NFT/Staking is rendered as one flat <div> with no purple highlighted Text segments. Modal cannot be opened because no terms are clickable. Also discovered: data-testid attributes in RN code are not reaching the DOM (RN web requires testID), and /api/atlas/chat returns 500 (falls back to /simple).

minor.issue: Returns HTTP 500 (SSE streaming). Frontend falls back to /api/atlas/chat/simple which works. Visible in browser console as 'Failed to load resource: 500' on every send.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL - Glossary terms (Bitcoin, Blockchain, DeFi, Wallet, NFT, Staking, etc.) are NOT highlighted in purple/underlined in assistant messages. Inspected bubble HTML shows a single flat <div> containing the entire response text without nested highlighted <Text> spans. The fetch /api/glossary/terms works (verified via direct fetch in browser returning 20 terms), but the component appears to render before terms state populates OR setTerms() does not trigger a re-render of msg content. Net result: feature completely non-functional in Atlas chat.
Cannot be verified end-to-end because no highlighted term is clickable (see above). Modal logic itself was not exercised in UI.

data-testid attributes are passed as JSX props to React Native components (TouchableOpacity/TextInput). React Native Web does NOT forward data-testid - it requires the testID prop which is then converted to data-testid in the DOM. As a result NONE of the testids (atlas-input, atlas-send-btn, atlas-quick-0..3, atlas-clear-btn, glossary-close-btn) exist in the rendered DOM, breaking automation/testability.

integration_issues.issue: Assistant message renders as plain text only. Terms list fetched successfully (count=20) but component output never contains highlighted children.

action_items: FIX GlossaryText: investigate why terms state is not segmenting the text. Likely candidates: (a) useEffect dependency on `language` runs but cachedTerms is shared module-level so the very first mount with language=undefined caches empty terms; (b) After streaming completes, msg.content updates but terms may already be empty array from a failed first render. Add console logs and verify setTerms is called.

REPLACE all `data-testid='...'` props in learn.tsx and GlossaryText.tsx with `testID='...'` so they survive React Native Web rendering. Affected: atlas-input, atlas-send-btn, atlas-quick-0..3, atlas-clear-btn, glossary-close-btn.
FIX /api/atlas/chat 500 error (SSE endpoint) or remove the SSE call if /simple is the intended path. Currently every chat triggers a noisy 500 in console.
After fix, re-test: send message in Atlas chat, verify purple underlined terms appear in assistant bubble, tap a term -> popup with definition + example, close via overlay AND via close button.
critical_code_review_comments: GlossaryText.tsx line 41: silent catch hides fetch failures - add console.warn to aid debugging.
GlossaryText.tsx uses module-level `let cachedTerms = {}` mutable state - acceptable but means cache cannot be invalidated when API updates.
GlossaryText.tsx case-insensitive substring matching may produce false positives inside words (e.g. 'gas' inside 'gasoline'). Consider word boundary check.
learn.tsx onSubmitEditing on multiline TextInput does not fire on Enter (multiline swallows Enter). Send only works via button tap; the button has no testID.
SSE parser in learn.tsx line 41 concatenates `data: <text>` chunks without preserving line breaks; could merge tokens incorrectly depending on server framing.
/api/glossary/terms unknown lang fallback returns ENGLISH (good), but `lang` is not validated; trivially accepts any string.
test_credentials: Not required for this feature; tested anonymously.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest covers FR/EN/ES + default + unknown-lang fallback. The Atlas chat lives at https://academy-preview-11.preview.emergentagent.com/learn (route is /learn on web). To trigger glossary terms in response, send: 'I'm a beginner. Define Bitcoin and Blockchain and DeFi in 3 short sentences each, no questions.' The AI then returns a long answer containing multiple terms - currently NONE are highlighted. Check bubble innerHTML to verify nested <span>/<Text> presence with computed color rgb(124,58,237).
rca of the issue: The bubble innerHTML observed during testing was a single flat <div class='css-text-146c3p1 ...'> containing the entire assistant text with no nested children, meaning the GlossaryText `segments.map` produced exactly one segment with term=null. Since a direct browser fetch confirmed /api/glossary/terms?lang=en returns 20 terms, the component fetch must either (a) not run for the right language, (b) fail silently (catch() => {}), or (c) succeed but setTerms triggers a re-render that does NOT re-flow inside the streaming message - possibly because React Native Web's <Text> component flattens nested Text into a single div when the parent re-renders mid-stream. Recommended first step: add logs in GlossaryText useEffect and segments useMemo to confirm terms.length at render time; if non-zero, the issue is RN Web Text flattening; if zero, the fetch never completed for that language.
